prompt: The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as which country?
Which ocean is the largest on Earth?
Lake Baikal holds about 20% of all the world's unfrozen fresh surface water. What is unusual about its age?
options: It was created by a meteor impact about 2 million years ago
It is roughly 25–30 million years old, making it the oldest lake on Earth
It is geologically young at only 500,000 years old
It formed during the last Ice Age, making it about 15,000 years old

prompt: Which continent has no permanent human residents and no countries?
China and Russia are tied for a peculiar world record: which one?
options: Most land borders with other countries (14 each)

prompt: Saudi Arabia is the largest country in the world with no permanent natural rivers. Which other Gulf state shares this distinction?
The Amazon and Nile rivers trade blows for 'world's longest'. Which river drains the largest watershed — collecting water from the most land area?
Which country is sometimes called the 'Land of a Thousand Lakes' — and actually has over 187,000 of them?
Lesotho, Vatican City, and San Marino share a rare geographical trait. What is it?
options: They are all smaller than a major city airport
They are all entirely surrounded by a single other country
They have no coastline and no rivers

prompt: The Atacama Desert in South America is so arid that some weather stations there have never recorded rainfall. On which coast of the continent does it sit?
options: It is landlocked in the interior

prompt: Mount Chimborazo in Ecuador is not the tallest mountain above sea level, yet its summit is the farthest point from Earth's centre. Why?
options: It sits on a high plateau that adds to its effective height
Earth bulges at the equator, giving equatorial peaks extra distance from the core
It was taller before significant erosion over millennia
Its base is below sea level, making its total rise larger

prompt: Which European country shares a land border with Morocco — not because of a sea crossing, but because it holds two small enclaves on the African continent?
The Equator passes through the territory of which of these countries?
Which country is both an entire continent and a single nation?
Mount Everest, the highest point on land, sits on the border between Nepal and which other country?
Which two continents does the Suez Canal separate?
Greenland, the world's largest island, is a self-governing territory of which country?
Which country has a maple leaf at the centre of its flag?
Istanbul is the only major city in the world that sits on two continents. Which two?
If you sailed due south from the southern tip of India across the open ocean, the first continent you'd hit is:
Turkey's largest city is Istanbul, but its capital is a more central city. Which one?
The Dead Sea has the lowest land elevation on Earth, with its shore over 400 metres below sea level. It borders Jordan and which other country/territory?
Which country's main territory is the only one to stretch across all four hemispheres — north and south of the Equator, and east and west of the International Date Line?
Russia covers more time zones than any other country. How many?
The Strait of Gibraltar connects the Mediterranean Sea to which body of water?
Italy is famously shaped like a boot, and at its 'toe' it nearly touches a large island. Which island is it?
Canada and which other country share the longest international land border in the world?
The deepest known point in any ocean is the Challenger Deep, nearly 11 km down. In which ocean trench is it found?
Which country's name, in its own language, means roughly 'Land of the Eagles' — and indeed features a double-headed eagle on its flag?
The Nile is unusual among major rivers because it flows in which overall direction?
options: Southward, emptying into the Indian Ocean
Eastward, emptying into the Red Sea

prompt: Point Nemo, the most remote spot in the world's oceans, is so far from land that the nearest humans are often astronauts aboard the ISS passing overhead. In which ocean does it lie?
